## Summary

Makes the `/revise` page demo fully interactive so visitors can experience the Revise Queue workflow firsthand.

**Before:** Right panel was hardcoded to item 1. Clicking queue items highlighted them but didn't change the detail view. Author controls did nothing.

**After:**
- All 7 queue items have full detail data (6-part diagnosis + 3 A/B/C proposals each)
- Clicking a queue item updates the entire right panel with that item's evidence, symptom, cause, fix direction, reader effect, mistake-proofing, and proposals
- A/B/C proposals are clickable — selected proposal gets a gold highlight + "Selected" badge
- Author controls (Accept/Keep original/Reject all/Write custom/Defer) save per-item decisions with visual feedback
- Decision badges (Accepted/Kept/Rejected/Custom/Deferred) appear on queue items in the left panel
- Footer counters update dynamically as decisions are made
- Auto-advances to next undecided item after making a decision
- "Try it!" call-to-action with pulse animation prompts users to interact

## Scope

**Touches:** `components/revise/ReviseQueueDemo.tsx` only (1 file)

**Does NOT touch:** workbench, evaluation pipeline, scoring, any backend routes

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Pure frontend demo component, no backend calls
- All sample data is hardcoded — no risk of affecting real manuscripts

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — marketing demo component only
